channel.title: Raleigh NC Parks and Recreation News
channel.description: Latest news from Raleigh Parks and Recreation

item.title: Historic Oakwood Neighborhood Trolley Tour (Mar 10, 2026)
item.description:
Explore landmark residences and learn about the influential families and visionary builders who shaped this enduring community. This hour-long tour invites people of all ages to explore, learn, and connect with the city’s rich past.



Location: Tour starts and ends at the Tucker House, 418 N. Person St.
Date: Sunday, April 12
Times: 1 p.m., 2 p.m., and 3 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $12
Purchase Trolley Tickets

item.title: Powell Drive Community Day (Mar 10, 2026)
item.description:
Friday night, we will host a meet and greet with refreshments. Swing by and meet Director Denise Hill and Assistant Director Jason Rodri and pick their brains about programs YOU want to see at Powell Drive.

On Saturday, we will be going all out with inflatables, music, food trucks, a free-throw contest, and more! We will also have many City of Raleigh program area representatives with exciting opportunities to share.

Stop on by for great family fun and enjoy all that the City has to offer!



Friday, April 17, 5 - 8 p.m.
Saturday, April 18, Noon - 4 p.m.
Cost: Free
Ages: All
Share your feedback about Powell Drive Park!

item.title: The Money Game Changer (Mar 2, 2026)
item.description:
This interactive workshop teaches essential money basics in an engaging and practical format. Participants will walk away with the confidence and tools they need to make smart financial decisions now and in the future.



We encourage parents to stay and listen, as this information is valuable for the whole family. Light refreshments will be served. Registration is required, and space is limited to 30 participants.

Location: Worthdale Community Center
Date: Wednesday, March 18
Time: 6 - 8 p.m. 
Ages: 13 - 17
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Scentsational Candle Making (Mar 2, 2026)
item.description:
Learn the basics of hand-poured candles, including choosing scents, working with wax safely, and creating a simple custom candle to take home.



This beginner-friendly workshop is perfect for anyone looking to try a new hobby or enjoy a creative afternoon. Cost is just $10 per person, and all supplies are included. No experience needed, just bring your creativity!



Location: Sanderford Road Park
Date: Saturday, March 21
Times: 1:30 - 3 p.m.
Ages: 18 and up
Cost: $10
Register on RecLink

item.title: Family Fun Day Space! (Mar 2, 2026)
item.description:
This year, we celebrate the 65th anniversary of Americans in space, as well as our upcoming return to the Moon with the Artemis Missions. You can try your hand at a simulated Extra Vehicular Activity (EVA) and launch a “Squeeze Bottle Straw Rocket” into orbit around a Lunar target. A limited number of NASA stickers and posters are available while supplies last.



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, March 14
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No Registration Required

item.title: Level Up! Mountain Bike Basics (Mar 17, 2026)
item.description:
Our certified instructors will guide you through the fundamentals of mountain biking. Learn and practice riding techniques, body position, braking, and shifting. 

The course will also cover trail etiquette and local mountain biking resources. We'll wrap up with a group trail ride, putting your new skills to use. Bikes and helmets are provided. 

Successful completion of the course qualifies you to participate in the Bike Loaner Program at Forest Ridge Park. Preregistration is required.



Location: Forest Ridge Park
Dates: Saturdays - April 18, May 16, Aug. 22
Time: 9-11 a.m. 
Ages: 14 and up
Cost: $26 Resident / $41 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Introduction to Honeybees and Beekeeping (Mar 9, 2026)
item.description:
The honeybee serves an important party in pollinating flowers, especially the flowers that produce our fruits and vegetables. They also provide us honey to eat. 

Come and learn about the honeybee, beekeeping as a hobby, and increase your awareness of this important pollinator in our midst. This is a family friendly program. 

There will be a presentation, hands on stations, and a Q&A at the end. If you an interest in the honeybee or beekeeping, then this is the program for you! Please register each person who plans to attend.



Location: Laurel Hills Park
Date: Saturday, March 28
Time: 10:30 a.m. - 12:30 p.m.
Ages: 9 and up
Cost: $5
Register on RecLink

item.title: Sunset Hike (Mar 9, 2026)
item.description:
We will hike along the northern shoreline of the park, traversing roots and rocks, to pause at the peninsula point for the sunset. With less light on our return trip, you will need a headlamp or flashlight to illuminate your path.



Friday, March 20, 6:30 - 8:30 p.m.
Friday, April 24, 7 - 9 p.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $6
Register on RecLink

item.title: Barwell Road Community Garden Open House (Mar 16, 2026)
item.description:
Take a stroll, check out the plots, and even pre-select your favorite bed before our official garden rental system launches. Bring your curiosity, maybe your green thumb and let’s get ready to grow together!



Location: Barwell Road Park - The Community Garden is located to the left of the entrance sign
Date: Saturday, April 11
Time: 1 - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No registration is required. Parking is available at the garden and near the playground.

item.title: Gather and Grow with Us at Our Urban Farm Open House (Feb 27, 2026)
item.description:
Operated and supported in partnership with Raleigh City Farm, our Urban Farm was created in 2025 as a way to grow and distribute fresh produce to members of the Raleigh community.



Join us at the open house to take a tour of the site in its early stages and learn about our big plans for what’s to come. Take home some free, fresh produce (while supplies last), chat with the farmers, learn about volunteer opportunities, and meet your neighbors at this drop-in opportunity to see all things going on with our Urban Farm!



Check in at the lobby of Marsh Creek Community Center when you arrive to start your tour experience. This is a rain-or-shine event.



Location: Marsh Creek Community Center 
Date: Thursday, March 19 
Time: 2 - 7:30 p.m. 
Ages: All 
Cost: Free

item.title: Historic Raleigh Trolley Tours (Feb 26, 2026)
item.description:
Hop aboard the trolley to tour downtown Raleigh and learn about the city's rich history. Tours are an hour long and leave from Mordecai Historic Park. 



Dates: Saturdays, March - December
Times: 11 a.m., 12 p.m., 1 p.m., and 2 p.m.
Cost: $10 for adults, $6 for seniors and youth (7-17)
Free for children 6 and under
Ages: All ages
Get Tickets on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Parks from Bond to Beyond (Feb 19, 2026)
item.description:
Join Raleigh Parks for a fun, family-friendly afternoon where you can learn about the progress on 23 Parks Bond projects across the city, meet the people behind the work, and see how a community’s vote becomes parks that serve us all.  



Enjoy food, music, games, arts activities, and a chance to win prizes in our event raffle! 



Learn what’s happening now, and what’s coming next.



Location: Dix Park, Greg Poole, Jr., All Faiths Chapel
Date: Thursday, March 26
Time: 4:30 - 7:30 p.m.
Ages: Family-friendly event
Cost: Free



Accommodations and interpretation services are available upon request. Please contact lauryn.kabrich@raleighnc.gov.

item.title: Resin Wine Glass Making is Back! (Feb 26, 2026)
item.description:
Biltmore Hills Community Center



News Published 2/26/2026 Updated 3/10/2026



In this program, you will create different media art projects that you can take home while socializing with your fellow community members. All materials will be provided unless the instructor specifies pre-purchased items.



Location: Biltmore Hills Community Center
Date: Wednesdays, March 11
Time: Noon - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $20 
Register on RecLink



Biltmore Hills Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: A New Nash: Kick-Off Party & Open House (Feb 18, 2026)
item.description:
Historic Nash Square is one of our Capital City’s original public open spaces!



Join us as we kick off the Nash Square Public Realm Plan to learn about its past and present, and to help shape its future. Raleigh Parks is excited to create a New Nash that reflects the unique needs and desires of the community. We welcome your input and look forward to collaborating with you!



This event is family-friendly and will include activities for people of all ages. Accommodations and translation services are available upon request. Please contact nashsquare@publicinput.com for requests and assistance.  



Learn more & participate in the planning process!



Location: Whiskey Kitchen (201 W. Martin Street, Raleigh, NC 27601)
Date: Thursday, Feb. 26 
Time: 5:30 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: 2026 Summer Concert Series (Feb 18, 2026)
item.description:
Each summer, local artists donate their talent and time to put on a great free show for the community. On select Sundays from now until Aug. 16, we will host groups performing a variety of different genres that will help set the tone for an awesome evening at these two beautiful facilities. 



May 31 - SWAG
June 7 - Blue Notes Jazz Band
July 12 - Invitation
July 19 - Thelonius
July 26 - Capital
Aug. 2 - The Holly Springs Community Band
Aug. 9 - Triangle Sax Ensemble
Aug. 23 - Carpe Diem Saxophone Quartet



June 21 - Eat a Peach
June 28 - Triangle Taiko
Aug. 16  - Bull City Trio



Sunday Evenings
Time: 6 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture with Cheetie Kumar (Mar 13, 2026)
item.description:
She has been profiled in The New York Times, Southern Living, and The Wall Street Journal, and many other national publications.



Join us for an in-depth Q&A on Cheetie’s career, food advocacy work, and upcoming projects in the Raleigh restaurant scene.

item.title: Wake County Senior Games (Mar 13, 2026)
item.description:
Senior Games are Olympic-style sporting events held at the local, state, and national levels across the United States, engaging more than 100,000 participants ages 50 and older each year. The Games offer the camaraderie and excitement of friendly competition while promoting health, wellness, and active lifestyles for older adults. Participants are inspired to stay active, challenge themselves, and connect with others in their community.



The Wake County Senior Games would not be possible without the time, energy, and dedication of our incredible volunteers. Their support helps create a welcoming and encouraging environment for participants. Most importantly, volunteers help bring joy, motivation, and plenty of smiles to the athletes who compete.



Volunteer opportunities for the upcoming season are now available, and we would love your help. Visit www.wcseniorgames.com/volunteer to view the full list of opportunities available from March through May. Each event listing includes a brief description of the volunteer role along with dates, times, and locations.



All volunteers will receive a T-shirt, and snacks will be provided at each event. Volunteers under 18 years of age must be accompanied by an adult. Please remember that a volunteer waiver must be completed and brought with you for each event.



For more information or questions, please contact us at 919-996-4735 or wakecountyseniorgames@gmail.com



Join us in supporting the Wake County Senior Games and help make a difference in the lives of active older adults in our community.



Dates: March 27 - May 19
Ages: 15 and up
Volunteer with Wake County Senior Games

item.title: School of Rock Performance (Mar 5, 2026)
item.description:
Bring your own chairs or blanket to enjoy this outdoor concert showcasing talented young local musicians playing a variety of music, perfect for all ages.



Visit the "musical petting zoo", where you can learn about and try real instruments with guidance from musicians!



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Date: Friday, April 24
Time: 5 - 7:30 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Letterland Days at Pullen Park (Mar 5, 2026)
item.description:
This annual celebration and partnership with the Wake County Public School System brings phonics to life, helping students engage with the characters and concepts that make learning to read fun.



These special evenings will feature interactive activities such as face painting, obstacle courses, jump ropes, bubble stations, and appearances by Clever Cat, Bouncy Ben, and Annie Apple.



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Dates: Monday, May 4, and Thursday, May 7
Time: 5 - 8 p.m.
Ages: Kindergarten - 2nd grade
Cost: Free Admission, Ride tickets $2
Buy Ride Tickets on RecLink

item.title: Lucky Leprechauns and Grandparents (Feb 25, 2026)
item.description:
This special event is a perfect opportunity for grandparents and grandkids to come together and find the gold at the end of the rainbow. Come and join us at Kiwanis Park for a goodie hunt, tasty treat, fun family movie, and themed crafts!

Experience the richness of intergenerational bonds through engaging activities. Go green with us and stop at all of the stations to enjoy a fun-filled afternoon!

Participants 12 and under will be the registrants and must be accompanied by an adult.

Location: Kiwanis Community Center
Date: Monday, March 16
Time: 4 - 6 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: LGBTQIA+ Film Series (Feb 25, 2026)
item.description:
Join us for our monthly program on the first and fourth Tuesday of the month. We will be showcasing films with diverse themes around sexuality and gender. This program is co-hosted with the Carolina Aging Alliance.

Seating is limited, so please register in person or register on RecLink.



Date: Tuesdays
Time: 2 - 4:15 p.m.
Age: 18 and older
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink



March's second film is "Flawless." An ultraconservative security guard suffers a debilitating stroke and is assigned to a rehabilitative program that includes singing lessons with the drag queen next door. Seating is limited, so please register in person or online.



April's first film is "Vita & Virginia." The fascinating true story of the love affair between socialite and popular author Vita Sackville-West and literary icon Virginia Woolf. Seating is limited, so please register in person or online.  



April's second film is "Breaking Fast" Mo, a practicing Muslim living in West Hollywood, is learning to navigate life post heartbreak. An All-American guy surprises Mo by offering to break fast with him during the holy month of Ramadan.

item.title: City of Raleigh Museum Animal Adoption Day (Feb 25, 2026)
item.description:
Come experience the love and connection that awaits—you might just find your perfect companion! This event is free and open to everyone, even if you just need some puppy love!

Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, March 7
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No registration is required

item.title: Teens Wanted: Build a Comic with Us! (Mar 12, 2026)
item.description:
Work with artist Wolly McNair (who's worked with Nike, Archie Comics, IDW Comics) and help shape Raleigh’s new emergency preparedness comic book.



Share your ideas and tell us what characters, colors, and symbols make you feel safe and represented. Then create your own comic character and bring your hero to life. Come hungry, get creative, and be part of a project designed by teens, for teens!



If you can't attend the event, please take our survey.



Location: Peach Road Park
Date: Saturday, March 28
Time: noon - 2 p.m.
Ages: Teens
Cost: Free
Reserve Your Spot

item.title: Spring Black Neighborhood Walking Tour (Mar 12, 2026)
item.description:
Be sure to register to join in the fun! Saturday tour times are 10 a.m. and 2 p.m. and Sunday tour time 2 p.m.

Dates & Times: March 21 - June 21
Saturdays, 10 a.m. & 2 p.m.
Sundays, 2 p.m. 
Ages: All
Cost: $10 per adult / $5 per child (5- 17)
Register on RecLink

item.title: Make-A-Zine Workshop! (Mar 12, 2026)
item.description:
Youth will explore papercrafts, collage, drawing, and writing to create personal pieces about their own interests and collaborative pieces with the class as a whole.

At the end of each program, the class will have a showcase of their work for parents/guardians to see their finished zines.

Location: John P. "Top" Green African American Cultural Center 
Date: Fridays, April 10, 17, and 24
Time: 5:30 - 7:30 p.m.
Ages: 9 - 14
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Joins Lights Out for Bird Migration (Mar 4, 2026)
item.description:
Each spring and fall, millions of migrating birds travel through the State of North Carolina, mostly under the cover of darkness. To help assure their safe passage, the City of Raleigh is joining efforts to protect migrating birds by going Lights Out.



Non-essential lighting in many City facilities is turned off between 11 p.m. and 6 a.m., lowering costs, reducing energy consumption, and supporting the City’s sustainability goals in the Community Climate Action Plan. Each spring and fall, this also benefits migrating birds! 



Bright lights can attract and disorient nocturnally migrating birds, leading to potential collisions with buildings. Migratory bird populations are in serious decline, and such collisions are significant contributors to these losses. Turning off lights is a simple way to make a positive impact!  



All are encouraged to join the Lights Out initiative to help protect migrating birds by turning off non-essential nighttime lighting through the month of May. Audubon North Carolina  provides these recommendations to help make the skies a little darker during migration seasons: 



Through these standard practices in many City buildings, Raleigh participates in Lights Out for Bird Migration twice yearly, in the spring and fall. Wake Audubon  is planning additional "Lights Out Wake" activities in partnership with this important effort. Details will be available on Wake Audubon's website.



For more information on Lights Out initiatives, bird migration forecasts, and the latest research developments, please visit the BirdCast website.  For nocturnal bird migration alerts in Raleigh, please visit the BirdCast Migration Alerts page  for the city, which provides a three-day forecast for bird migration.



The lands and waters managed by Raleigh Parks form the backbone of a resilient, citywide ecological framework. Learn more about what Raleigh is doing in the Environmental Resilience section of the Raleigh Parks Plan.

item.title: Beginner's Crochet Class (Mar 4, 2026)
item.description:
This relaxed, beginner-friendly class is a great introduction to the basics. You’ll learn how to hold your hook, work a few simple stitches, and get comfortable with yarn at your own pace.



We’ll focus on building confidence and enjoying the process while you create small, approachable starter pieces. No prior experience required—just an interest in learning something new. All materials are provided, so you can simply show up and start stitching.



Location: Laurel Hills Community Center
Date: Tuesdays starting April 7
Time: 6:30 - 7:30 p.m
Ages: 12 and older
Cost: $25 Resident/$40 Non-Resident 
Register on RecLink

item.title: Improvements Are Coming to Walnut Creek Wetland Park (Mar 4, 2026)
item.description:
It’s official: a great new community space is coming to Walnut Creek Wetland Park along Bailey Drive. Raleigh City Council has approved the construction contract, moving the project from plan to action. 



The Bailey Drive Gateway will make the park easier to access and more inviting for the community. Improvements include: 



Construction will be led by Harrod and Associates Constructors Inc. and is expected to be complete by Winter 2027. The project is funded jointly by Raleigh Parks, The Conservation Fund, Raleigh Department of Transportation, and the Outdoor Recreation Legacy Partnership. 



Learn more: Walnut Creek Wetland Park Bailey Drive Gateway

item.title: Ready, Set, GLOW! (Mar 4, 2026)
item.description:
Young EGG-plorers will venture into designated areas for a captivating search of luminous eggs and prizes. While the music sounds, youngsters can move around collecting eggs, but like musical chairs, each time the music stops, participants must freeze in place. Once the music strikes back into action, they can continue in search of goodies.



Let’s LIGHT up the night with a dazzling hunt and SHELL-ebration! This will be a night to shine and is sure to spark fun with electrifying themed crafts, interactive games, radiant face painting and dynamic dance moves. Dress in your most vivid attire and bolt over to this lit event that’s sure to be a hopping good time!



Location: John Chavis Memorial Park
Date: Friday, March 27
Time: 6 - 8 p.m.
Ages: 6 - 11
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Spring EGG-Stravaganza at Chavis Park (Mar 4, 2026)
item.description:
Young EGG-explorers will be divided into two age groups (0-3 years and 4-5 years) in search of bright, colorful eggs tucked away in designated areas of the park. Joyful EGG-pressions are sure to bring cheer as little chicks fill their baskets with goodies and prizes.



Families will also EGG-perience spring crafts, free carousel rides, and other festive activities designed to EGG-cite and delight kids of all ages. Color us excited to host this EGG-stra special Hoppening SHELL-ebration that is sure to bring smiles and fun times.



Date: Friday, March 27
Time: 10 a.m. - noon 
Ages: 0-5
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Join us for Gardening Workshops (Mar 2, 2026)
item.description:
From Seed to Bloom! This hands-on workshop is focused on flower propagation from seed, designed to educate, inspire, and grow your confidence! We will cover germination, fertilizing, transplanting, continuing care, and seed collection. You’ll also receive advanced tips, tricks, and recommendations for long-blooming flowers suited for our area. We will start with several varieties of annual flowers for you to take home and enjoy.



Whether you're a beginner eager to start your garden journey or a seasoned gardener looking for budget-friendly propagation methods, this workshop is for you!

item.title: Tuesdays in the Square (Feb 24, 2026)
item.description:
A pop-up series to explore the Nash Square Public Realm Plan on the first and third Tuesdays of March and April.

Staff will be available from 11 a.m. - 1 p.m., with 15-minute tours focusing on the week's theme at 12 p.m. and 12:30 p.m. 

Everyone is welcome to share their experiences. See you at Nash Square!



Location: Nash Square
Time: 11 a.m. - 1 p.m., tours at 12 p.m. and 12:30 p.m. 
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No registration is required

item.title: Travel With Us: New Active Adult Day Trips Announced (Feb 23, 2026)
item.description:
Step into the 40,000-square-foot Billy Graham Library and explore the extraordinary life and legacy of one of the most influential public figures of the 20th century. This unforgettable visit takes you on a journey from Billy Graham’s humble farm beginnings to his role on the world stage as an advisor to presidents, a global traveler, and a voice heard by millions.



Nestled on 20 beautifully landscaped acres in Charlotte, North Carolina, just miles from his childhood home, the barn-shaped library offers a compelling blend of history, personal storytelling, and cultural impact. Journey at your leisure through immersive multimedia exhibits, photographs, and memorabilia, and you’ll gain insight into the life, times, and far-reaching influence of a man who helped shape modern American history.
- Charter motorcoach transportation
- Lunch on your own Dairy Barn
- General Admission Ticket



Time: 8:15 a.m. to 6 p.m.
Cost: $81/Residents $96/Non-residents
Additional Information
Trip Registration



Cedar Creek Gallery is a haven for art lovers year-round, but it truly comes alive during the Spring Pottery & Glass Festival. Watch nationally recognized artists transform raw materials into stunning works of art right before your eyes, and enjoy an up-close look at their techniques and creative processes through glass blowing, pottery, wood turning, and more.



In addition to incredible artist demonstrations, guests can wander through the beautiful gardens, purchase plants, and receive tips and advice from knowledgeable horticulturalists. Explore the gallery featuring the work of over 200 local and national artists and round out the day with live music and delicious offerings from food trucks.
- Lunch on your own at the food trucks



Time: 9:30 a.m. to 2 p.m.
Cost: $20/Residents $35/Non-residents
Additional Information
Trip Registration



Travel to Sanford, NC, Temple Theatre. Bright Star is a musical about a literary editor and a World War II soldier who discover a secret that changes their lives. The show is set in the American South in the 1920s and 1940s. When Alice Murphy meets a young soldier just home from World War II, he awakens her longing for the child she once lost. Haunted by their unique connection, Alice sets out on a journey to understand her past—and what she finds has the power to transform both of their lives.
- Lunch on your own downtown Sanford.

Time: 10:30 a.m. to 5 p.m.
Cost: $53/Residents $68/Non-residents
Additional Information
Trip Registration



Visit our website to learn more about our day and multi-day travel opportunities.

item.title: Raleigh Adult Athletics Registration (Feb 18, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks Athletics is offering adult softball, baseball, and kickball for ages 18 and up. Programs will be offered at various park locations throughout the city. Registration will be held from February 15-28. Interested athletes may register online or at any City of Raleigh community center. Adult leagues register as a team. Individuals who are interested in playing but are not currently part of a team need to contact the Athletics office to be placed on our Free Agent List.



For more information, please call 919-996-6836 or email Athletics@raleighnc.gov.



Registration Dates: Feb. 15-28
Cost: Varies
Ages: 18 and older
Register on RecLink
